ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource allocation entry */,
  "src" INTEGER /* Source node identifier */,
  "dst" INTEGER /* Destination node identifier */,
  "spec" INTEGER /* Specification type of the connection */,
  "slots_used" INTEGER /* Number of slots currently used */,
  "core_src" INTEGER /* Core source slot identifier */,
  "core_dst" INTEGER /* Core destination slot identifier */,
  "tfk_slot" INTEGER /* TFK slot identifier */,
  "allocation_date" TEXT /* Date the resource was allocated */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date the resource allocation expires */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the resource allocation (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_resources_dst FOREIGN KEY ("dst") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_resources_spec FOREIGN KEY ("spec") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_resources_src FOREIGN KEY ("src") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node (e.g., Router1) */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of node (e.g., Router, Switch) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the node */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the node was installed */,
  "node_status" TEXT /* Status of the node (e.g., online, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification */,
  "spec_name" TEXT /* Name of the specification */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specification */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth of the specification (e.g., Mbps) */,
  "latency" REAL /* Latency of the specification (e.g., ms) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "slots" (
  "slot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each slot category */,
  "slot_type" TEXT /* Type of slot (e.g., Core Slot, Regular Slot) */,
  "total_slots" INTEGER /* Total number of slots available */,
  "used_slots" INTEGER /* Number of slots currently in use */,
  "slot_status" TEXT /* Status of the slot (e.g., available, reserved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("slot_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user within the system */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the resource */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES network_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource the metric is associated with */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of performance metric (e.g., latency, throughput) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the metric was recorded */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., ms, Mbps) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES network_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource related to the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., bandwidth usage, connection failure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the alert was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES network_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the event occurred */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., user action, system error) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the event */,
  "event_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event (e.g., critical, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, update) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical entry */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource the historical data is associated with */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the historical event occurred */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., allocation, deallocation) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the historical event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_history_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES network_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "slot_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage entry */,
  "slot_id" INTEGER /* ID of the slot the usage data is associated with */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* When the usage data was recorded */,
  "usage_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the slot that is being used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_slot_usage_slot_id FOREIGN KEY ("slot_id") REFERENCES slots ("slot_id")
)
